title: README example 'let ok = key.verify(...)' — ok is () not bool
description: wolftpm/README.md line 137: 'let ok = key.verify(&message_hash, &sig)?;' — verify returns Result<(), Error>; after '?', ok is (). The variable name 'ok' implies a bool return but the return is unit. This would generate an unused-variable warning if compiled. A user reading the README will be confused about the return type. Fix: drop the 'let ok =' binding entirely, or replace with a comment showing how to handle the SignatureInvalid case.
